title: Invertibility condition for a matrix
instruction: Without changing their meanings, convert each of the following sentences into a sentence having the form “$P$ if and only if $Q$.”
contents: For matrix $A$ to be invertible, it is necessary and sufficient that $\det(A) \neq 0$.

title: Constant derivative implies linearity
instruction: Without changing their meanings, convert each of the following sentences into a sentence having the form “$P$ if and only if $Q$.”
contents: If a function has a constant derivative then it is linear, and conversely.

title: Zero product property

title: Rationality of multiples

title: Adventure Occurrence Condition
instruction: Without changing their meanings, convert each of the following sentences into a sentence having the form “$P$ if and only if $Q$.”
contents: For an occurrence to become an adventure, it is necessary and sufficient for one to recount it. (Jean-Paul Sartre)

title: Truth values of propositional variables
contents: Suppose the statement $((P \wedge Q) \vee R) \Rightarrow (R \vee S)$ is false. Find the truth values of $P, Q, R$ and $S$. (This can be done without a truth table.)

title: Truth values of R and S given a logical equivalence
contents: Suppose $P$ is false and that the statement $(R \Rightarrow S) \Leftrightarrow (P \wedge Q)$ is true. Find the truth values of $R$ and $S$. (This can be done without a truth table.)
